commands.review_summary:
The PR normalizes runtime tool schema projection for nullable and literal-union shapes, preserves explicit null clear sentinels during tool argument validation, and adds cron/provider compatibility regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +83, Tests +143. Total +226 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main returns raw TypeBox nullable/composed cron schemas through the provider/Codex projection path while strict provider adapters reject those shapes. I did not run a live failing provider request in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the review found no blocking findings; this maintainer-labeled PR should proceed through normal maintainer gates.

target.title: fix(agents): project nullable tool schemas for providers
target.body:
## Summary

- Project nullable and literal-union tool schemas into the OpenAPI-friendly provider schema shape used for runtime tools.
- Preserve runtime validation semantics for explicit `null` clear sentinels before provider projection.
- Cover the cron tool schema path that was failing current-main provider-compat checks.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 packages/llm-core/src/validation.ts packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json packages/llm-core/src/validation.ts packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- AWS Crabbox changed gate: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_cbb1c631efeb`, run `run_4415ced3845b`, command `pnpm check:changed`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, exit 0.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: strict provider adapters should not receive `anyOf`/`oneOf`/`const`/null-type composition from runtime tool schemas when a provider-safe projection is available; runtime validation should still preserve explicit `null` clear sentinels.

Real environment tested: local Codex worktree for focused Vitest/format/lint proof, plus AWS Crabbox Linux remote changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/llm-core/src/validation.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`.

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 3 shards with 114 tests; remote `pnpm check:changed` completed successfully in run `run_4415ced3845b`.

Observed result after fix: projected cron schemas expose provider-compatible plain `string`/`array`/`enum` shapes while raw tool validation keeps `agentId`, `sessionKey`, and `payload.toolsAllow` as `null` when the schema accepts clear sentinels.

What was not tested: live Telegram or full assistant-turn E2E; this PR is limited to runtime schema projection and argument validation contracts.
target.author: vincentkoc
